sources.notes: Statement and constraints: maintain two fully dynamic undirected graphs A and B under edge toggles and output the minimum number of edges to add to A so that A includes B.
Official tutorial page contains the accepted solution code. The code converts edge toggles into active intervals, processes them on a time segment tree, and uses rollback DSU for A and for A union B.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution candidate; not consulted for this review.

annotation.summary: Maintain two dynamic graphs A and B. After each edge toggle, compute the minimum extra edges needed in A so every connected component of B lies inside one connected component of A.
annotation.constraints: n and q are up to 4e5, with arbitrary edge insertions and deletions, so online recomputation of connected components is impossible.
annotation.core_idea: For a fixed time, first contract the components of A. To make A include B, every B component spanning c current A-components needs c-1 added edges. This total equals components(A) - components(A union B). The dynamic part is handled offline: turn every toggled edge into its active time intervals, insert each interval into a segment tree over query time, and DFS that tree. During recursion, maintain two rollback DSUs, one for A edges only and one for all active A/B edges. At a leaf, output A.comps - united.comps, then rollback all changes when returning.
annotation.complexity: O(q log q log n) worst-case with rollback DSU by size and no path compression; memory O(q log q).
tricks: answer formula components(A) - components(A union B)
convert edge toggles to active intervals

solution_variants.name: offline-dynamic-connectivity-rollback-dsu
solution_variants.summary: Build active intervals for every edge, add those intervals to a segment tree over time, and recursively maintain rollback DSUs for A and A union B to answer each query leaf.

tags.evidence: The main task is maintaining connected components of graphs under edge insertions and deletions.
tags.description: Maintain or answer graph connectivity information under edge insertions, deletions, or time-varying edge sets.
tags.created_reason: This is a classic reusable retrieval target distinct from static connectivity; existing graph/connectivity is too broad and rollback DSU is an implementation detail.

tags.evidence: The DFS over time intervals must undo all DSU parent, size, and component-count changes after leaving a segment-tree node.
Queries are processed by divide-and-conquer over the time axis using a segment tree of active intervals.
Edge toggles are not answered by an online fully dynamic data structure; all queries are converted to active intervals before processing.
